events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Scenario: commercial construction, Locates & marking, observed field condition: mechanical digging inside tolerance zone. Work rough-complete. Reference 811/CGA best practices. Need determine compliance.

811/CGA best practices: tolerance zone is a defined horizontal space from outside wall of underground facility. Typically 18 inches to 24 inches on each side depending state. Within tolerance zone, only hand digging or vacuum excavation allowed, no mechanical excavation. Mechanical digging inside tolerance zone is non-compliant and dangerous (high/critical risk). Decision must be fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Component conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant? The work is Locates & marking, but observed condition is mechanical digging inside tolerance zone. The locating/marking maybe not compliant because excavation within tolerance zone violates. Component conditions could include non-compliant. Maybe installed-defective? Since the locate/marking maybe not installed? The scenario says work under review is Locates & marking, observed field condition mechanical digging inside tolerance zone. The locating and marking may be present but excavation practice violates tolerance zone. So component condition non-compliant. Could also installed-defective? Not exactly component defective. Use non-compliant.

Need fill fields: risk high or critical? Mechanical digging inside tolerance zone can strike utilities, life safety, so critical maybe. But typical benchmark maybe high. Let's assess. 811 tolerance zone violation is high risk of utility strike, could be critical in commercial project. The instructions: if dangerous or non-compliant, decision fail. Risk maybe high. We'll choose high or critical? Need decide. Many inspectors would rate critical because potential gas/electric strike. But maybe high. Let's set critical? The schema includes critical. Mechanical digging inside tolerance zone is a serious hazard. I'd choose critical.

percent_complete: rough-complete, so maybe 80? Work is roughed in but not closed out. For locates & marking, maybe 90? Use 80.

value: 0 (no numeric reading). sound_source, event_time, rate, order irrelevant but must include. Use defaults: sound_source "", event_time 0.0, rate 0.0, order [].

workable: [] unless asked. findings: ["Mechanical excavation observed within the tolerance zone, violating hand-dig/vacuum-only requirements."] actions: ["Stop mechanical excavation within tolerance zone.", "Expose utilities by hand digging or vacuum excavation within tolerance zone per 811/CGA best practices.", "Verify locate marks and retrain crew."] rationale: "811/CGA best practices prohibit mechanical excavation within the tolerance zone of underground utilities; only non-mechanical methods are permitted. Observed mechanical digging inside the zone creates a strike hazard and is non-compliant." references: ["811/CGA best practices - tolerance zone / hand digging requirements", "state one-call laws"].

Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Mechanical excavation is being performed inside the tolerance zone, violating the hand-dig/vacuum-only requirement for protecting underground utilities."],
  "actions": ["Immediately stop mechanical excavation within the tolerance zone.", "Expose and work around utilities using hand digging or vacuum excavation only within the tolerance zone.", "Verify locate marks, reassess the excavation plan, and retrain personnel on 811/CGA tolerance-zone requirements."],
  "rationale": "811/CGA best practices define a tolerance zone around marked underground utilities where only non-mechanical excavation is permitted. Mechanical digging inside this zone creates a high probability of utility strike and is non-compliant.",
  "references": ["811/CGA best practices - tolerance zone and hand digging requirements", "Applicable state one-call / utility damage prevention laws"]
}
